text: Science has a clearer story about serial interval than social media does. But what exactly is serial interval? It's the time between the onset of symptoms in a primary case and a secondary case. Imagine a virus spreading. The serial interval helps us understand how quickly it jumps from person to person. Studies show serial intervals vary—sometimes just a few days, other times longer. It's complex. Understanding this helps public health officials predict outbreaks and implement better control measures. Want more science insights? Hit that like button and subscribe for more!
